message:
fix(sandbox): resolve macOS symlink path mismatches and harden egress/WASI validation

Four correctness fixes in fcp-sandbox and minor cleanups:

1. egress.rs: Skip unparseable CIDR deny rules with a warning instead
   of hard-failing. Previously a typo in the deny list would block ALL
   egress traffic. Now invalid CIDRs are logged and skipped so that
   one misconfigured rule does not become a total outage.

2. macos.rs: Canonicalize policy paths before starts_with comparison.
   macOS symlinks /tmp → /private/tmp and /var → /private/var, which
   caused the sandbox to deny access to paths that should have been
   allowed by writable_paths and readonly_paths policy entries.

3. sandbox.rs: Canonicalize temp_dir in path-resolution tests to match
   the production canonicalization behavior, fixing spurious test
   failures on macOS where /var/folders is really /private/var/folders.

4. wasi.rs: Fix LEB128 test — [0x80, 0x02] encodes 256, not 128;
   the correct encoding of 128 is [0x80, 0x01]. Also fix the HTTP
   access validation test: host_allow constraints are host-based, not
   method-based, so GET to an allowed host should succeed.

Also: add #[doc(panics)] to SignedFcpsFrame::verify in fcp-protocol,
and apply rustfmt import reorder to apple-reminders client.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
